evals.prompt: I have an agent on the platform, can you set up evals for it?
evals.expected_output: Inspects agent, checks callback for variable handling, creates goldens + sims

assertions.text: Uses inspect-app.py or equivalent to inspect the agent
References the interview guide or asks about requirements
Creates golden YAML for deterministic flows
Creates simulation YAML for open-ended flows
Checks before_agent_callback for derived vs override-safe variables

evals.prompt: run the evals and tell me whats broken
evals.expected_output: Uses run-all-evals.py, triages with triage-results.py, reads transcripts

assertions.text: Uses run-all-evals.py to run all eval types
Uses triage-results.py to categorize failures
Reads transcripts before attributing failures to flakiness
Distinguishes platform issues from agent issues
Generates a combined or iteration report

evals.prompt: profanity escalation is failing, tools are missing
evals.expected_output: Reads transcript, checks tool availability, uses trigger pattern

assertions.text: Reads the actual failure transcript before proposing fixes
Checks whether the tool is in the agent's tool list
Does NOT add hardcoded phrase matching to callbacks
Follows LLM detects / callbacks execute principle
Re-runs with --runs 5

evals.prompt: i already have evals on the platform, help me set up a proper suite
evals.expected_output: Detects bootstrap entry point, pulls existing evals, preserves them

assertions.text: Detects 'existing app with evals' entry point
Pulls existing evals from platform before creating new ones
Presents existing evals to user for review
Does NOT delete existing platform evals
Generates TDD with both existing and new evals

evals.prompt: should the KB troubleshooting flow be a golden or sim?
evals.expected_output: Recommends sim because KB returns varying results

assertions.text: Recommends simulation, not golden
Explains KB returns varying results
References the decision criteria from interview guide
Suggests golden only for deterministic parts (routing)

evals.prompt: goldens failing but agent is doing the right thing, scores are 2/4
evals.expected_output: Confirms agent correct, suggests app-thresholds.py, last resort

assertions.text: Confirms agent behavior correct before threshold changes
Uses app-thresholds.py
Explains thresholds are a last resort
Suggests lowering similarity from 3 to 2

evals.prompt: the escalation eval is failing on text, can you look at this?
evals.expected_output: Identifies multi-model-call pattern, recommends after_model_callback

assertions.text: Identifies silent tool calls / multi-model-call pattern
Recommends after_model_callback
Mentions text_or_transcript() for audio safety
Mentions callback_context.events for cross-call text check
References gecx-design-guide

evals.prompt: requirements changed, spanish speakers should go to a bilingual agent now instead of escalating. update the evals
evals.expected_output: Updates TDD first, modifies affected evals, adds new ones

assertions.text: Updates TDD before modifying evals
Identifies which existing evals are affected
Modifies golden to match new routing
Adds new evals for untested flows
Runs affected evals after updating

evals.prompt: help me with my agent
evals.expected_output: Asks clarifying question

assertions.text: Asks a clarifying question
Offers build/run/debug options

evals.prompt: i changed a callback, what do i need to do
evals.expected_output: Mentions sync-callbacks.py, hook auto-sync, update tests, run tests

assertions.text: Mentions sync-callbacks.py
Mentions post-agent-update hook auto-syncs
Says to add or update tests
Says to run callback tests

evals.prompt: the profanity eval keeps failing, agent redirects instead of escalating
evals.expected_output: Identifies guideline vs taskflow conflict

assertions.text: Identifies the conflict between guideline and taskflow step
Explains LLM may prioritize guideline over taskflow
Recommends narrowing the guideline, not removing it
Does NOT add keyword detection in callbacks

evals.prompt: i added a counter for unintelligible responses but now its worse
evals.expected_output: Identifies counter as anti-pattern, recommends natural language

assertions.text: Identifies state-tracked counting reduces reliability
Recommends 'On the FIRST... On the SECOND...' pattern
References simpler instructions principle
Does NOT recommend adding more complexity

evals.prompt: agent sometimes says please hold and sometimes just silently ends, look at the trace
evals.expected_output: Recommends after_model_callback with events API

assertions.text: Recommends after_model_callback to inject text before end_session
Warns about multi-model-call turns
Recommends callback_context.events for cross-call check
Recommends text_or_transcript() for audio
Does NOT just say 'add text to instruction'

evals.prompt: payload update tool is getting called with empty args, look at this
evals.expected_output: Defense in depth: docstrings + state fallback + trigger pattern

assertions.text: Recommends better docstrings with (REQUIRED)
Recommends tool-level state fallback
Recommends trigger pattern as backup
Does NOT recommend hide_tool()

evals.prompt: auth routing golden failing, agent says cant find account
evals.expected_output: Checks session params override callback-derived variables

assertions.text: Checks whether session params override callback-derived variables
Identifies overriding derived variables short-circuits callback
Recommends reading before_agent_callback source
Recommends removing the override

evals.prompt: troubleshooting golden keeps failing, different text each run but tools pass
evals.expected_output: Convert to sim, don't loosen golden

assertions.text: Recommends converting to simulation, not loosening golden
Explains KB responses inherently vary
Suggests golden for deterministic parts only
Does NOT suggest lowering threshold as the fix

evals.prompt: agent sometimes forgets to call end_session after auth check fails, should i make the instruction stronger?
evals.expected_output: Move to callback, not stronger instruction

assertions.text: Identifies as callback territory (flag check + fixed response)
Explains why: no LLM judgment needed = callback
Recommends before_agent_callback or before_model_callback
Does NOT recommend adding MUST/ALWAYS to instruction

evals.prompt: agent is mixing up billing and troubleshooting flows, instruction is 4000 words
evals.expected_output: Split into multi-agent with root router + sub-agents

assertions.text: Recommends multi-agent architecture
Explains instruction following degrades with length
Isolates each domain into sub-agents
References gecx-design-guide architecture guidance

evals.prompt: escalation works 80% of the time, look at this trace
evals.expected_output: Trigger pattern: LLM sets state, callback returns tools

assertions.text: Recommends trigger pattern
Explains LLM detects / callback executes separation
State-setting tool must be in agent's tool list
Recommends after_model recovery
Does NOT just say 'make instruction clearer'

evals.prompt: i removed a turn from my golden yaml and pushed but the platform still has the old turns
evals.expected_output: Explains update_evaluation merges turns, must delete+recreate

assertions.text: Identifies that update_evaluation() merges turns, doesn't replace them
Recommends deleting the eval and recreating it to truncate
Does NOT recommend just pushing again or using a different update method

evals.prompt: my scenario evals show 0% in audio mode but they pass in text mode
evals.expected_output: Explains audio scoring differences: --audio flag, expect_criteria, evaluation_status

assertions.text: Explains that taskCompleted is broken in audio mode for scenarios
Recommends using --audio flag for scenario scoring
Mentions expect_criteria (LLM judges) over expect_tools for audio
Clarifies that goldens use evaluation_status directly, not --audio

evals.prompt: i cant push my agent code, something keeps blocking it
evals.expected_output: Identifies hook as the blocker, reads lint output, fixes the errors

assertions.text: Identifies that a pre-push hook is blocking the operation
Checks which hook blocked (lint or drift detection)
Reads the lint/error output to understand what needs fixing
Recommends fixing the underlying issue, not bypassing the hook

evals.prompt: we made a bunch of changes to fix the escalation, how do we track what we did
evals.expected_output: Uses run-and-report.py to run evals + generate report in one command

assertions.text: Recommends run-and-report.py as the iteration command
Includes --message flag for documenting what changed
Mentions --auto-revert for automatic rollback on regression

evals.prompt: i have a PRD for a customer support agent, build it
evals.expected_output: Interview for requirements, write TDD, wait for approval, then build

assertions.text: Routes to Build sub-skill
Starts with interview/requirements gathering, not building
Writes a TDD before creating anything
Waits for user approval of TDD before building
References the interview guide for the process

evals.prompt: my get_subscriber tool test is failing, response path not found
evals.expected_output: Routes to debug, checks if tool schema changed, fixes assertion

assertions.text: Routes to Debug sub-skill
Checks if the tool's response schema changed
Recommends updating the jsonpath in the test expectation
Does NOT recommend changing the tool itself as the first fix

evals.prompt: write callback tests for this agent
evals.expected_output: Uses sync-callbacks.py, follows test pattern with mocks, covers per-type guidance

assertions.text: Uses sync-callbacks.py to pull callback code from platform
Follows the mock injection test pattern (import python_code, MagicMock)
Covers what to test per callback type (before_agent, before_model, after_model)
Creates symlinks from agents/ to tests/

evals.prompt: sim says task not completed but the agent handled it fine
evals.expected_output: Success criteria too narrow, add 'X counts as successful'

assertions.text: Identifies success criteria as too narrow
Recommends adding 'X counts as a successful outcome' to task description
Does NOT recommend changing agent behavior

evals.prompt: agent transfers to billing when it should just escalate, look at this
evals.expected_output: Handle action entirely in before_model_callback to prevent routing

assertions.text: Identifies that LLM is routing AND acting simultaneously
Recommends handling the action entirely in before_model_callback
Explains that the callback prevents the LLM from making routing decisions alongside the action
Does NOT recommend just adding 'do not transfer' to the instruction

evals.prompt: escalation works from root but not when user is in the billing agent
evals.expected_output: Root callbacks don't fire on sub-agents, add callback to all agents

assertions.text: Identifies that root agent callbacks don't fire when user is on a sub-agent
Recommends adding the same callback to all agents that handle user messages
Does NOT recommend routing back to root for escalation

evals.prompt: all my scores pass but the eval still says FAIL, look at this
evals.expected_output: Platform scoring inconsistency, use triage-results.py to identify

assertions.text: Identifies this as a platform scoring inconsistency, not an agent issue
References triage-results.py SCORES_PASS_BUT_FAIL category
Explains the hallucination scorer can cause this even when disabled
Does NOT recommend changing the agent to fix a platform bug

evals.prompt: i want to build an agent that helps customers with their orders
evals.expected_output: Should start an interview to gather requirements, not jump to building

assertions.text: Routes to Build sub-skill
Asks clarifying questions about what the agent should do (not just starts building)
Asks about requirements source (PRD, spec doc, or interview)
Does NOT write a TDD or create an app before understanding requirements

evals.prompt: profanity escalation doesnt work, agent just says it cant process the request
evals.expected_output: Guardrails intercept before agent instructions — lower threshold or adjust guardrail config

assertions.text: Identifies guardrails as intercepting before agent instructions execute
Recommends checking/adjusting guardrail configuration (threshold or safe categories)
Does NOT recommend changing the agent instruction as the primary fix

evals.prompt: agent keeps suggesting restart wifi even though the KB tool didnt return that step
evals.expected_output: Example phrases in instruction cause hallucination — remove them

assertions.text: Identifies that example phrases in the instruction are being echoed as real steps
Recommends removing example troubleshooting phrases from the instruction
Explains agent should only suggest steps grounded in tool output
Does NOT recommend adding more examples or a longer instruction

evals.prompt: sim user keeps bringing up billing when the test is about troubleshooting
evals.expected_output: Task description too vague — add 'MUST cooperate fully' and restrict topics

assertions.text: Identifies the sim task description as too vague
Recommends adding directive language like 'MUST cooperate fully' or 'Do NOT bring up other topics'
Does NOT recommend changing the agent to handle off-topic sim behavior

evals.prompt: my sim eval keeps timing out before finishing in audio mode
evals.expected_output: max_turns too low for audio — increase by 4-6 turns

assertions.text: Identifies max_turns as too low
Recommends increasing max_turns, noting audio needs 4-6 extra turns
Does NOT recommend shortening the agent's responses as the fix

evals.prompt: i cleaned up the instruction to be shorter and now goldens went from 90% to 40%
evals.expected_output: Wholesale rewrites lose context the LLM relies on — revert and make targeted edits

assertions.text: Identifies wholesale rewrite as the cause of regression
Recommends reverting to the original and making small targeted edits instead
Explains the LLM relies on verbose context, examples, and phrasing
Does NOT recommend further simplification

evals.prompt: i captured the agents responses and made them into goldens, they pass but sims fail on the same flow
evals.expected_output: Circular testing — goldens should be ideal PRD behavior, not captured behavior

assertions.text: Identifies captured transcripts as circular testing
Explains goldens should represent ideal PRD behavior, not current agent behavior
Recommends rewriting goldens from the PRD/requirements

evals.prompt: root agent says empathy once but billing agent keeps being empathetic every turn
evals.expected_output: Sub-agent instruction contradicts root — align them

assertions.text: Identifies contradiction between root and sub-agent instructions
Recommends aligning sub-agent instruction with root agent policy
Does NOT recommend changing the root agent's policy to match the sub-agent

evals.prompt: golden fails randomly on tool calls even though all the right tools are called, look at this
evals.expected_output: Auxiliary tool reordering — remove non-core tools from golden expectations

assertions.text: Identifies LLM reordering auxiliary tools as the cause of flaky failures
Recommends removing auxiliary/classification tool calls from golden expectations
Recommends keeping only core behavior tools (routing, escalation, diagnostic)
Does NOT recommend fixing the tool call order in the instruction

evals.prompt: agent keeps checking for home internet even when customer has a phone issue
evals.expected_output: Negative condition in trigger confuses LLM — use positive triggers, separate step

assertions.text: Identifies the negative condition ('NOT home internet') in the trigger as the problem
Recommends using positive triggers only
Recommends putting the excluded category as a separate earlier step

evals.prompt: i built the agent, how do i verify its working before writing evals
evals.expected_output: Run the 7 verification gates from build-verification.md

assertions.text: References the build verification gates
Mentions verification steps like lint, agent hierarchy check, tool associations, smoke tests
Says to complete verification before writing evals

evals.prompt: auth routing golden is failing, agent routes to billing instead of troubleshoot. should i update the golden?
evals.expected_output: Fix the agent routing, do NOT update the golden to expect billing

assertions.text: Identifies the agent routing as wrong (not the eval)
Recommends fixing the agent instruction or routing logic
Does NOT recommend updating the golden to match the wrong behavior
Explains goldens represent the contract with the user — weakening them defeats their purpose

evals.prompt: outage golden is failing but the agent response looks correct, the test data changed
evals.expected_output: Update the golden — agent is correct, eval expectation is stale

assertions.text: Reads the transcript and confirms agent behavior is correct
Identifies the eval expectation as stale (test data changed)
Recommends updating the golden expected text to match the new correct behavior
Does NOT recommend changing the agent to match the old expectation

evals.prompt: we just fixed the escalation callback to use standard text, but the golden still expects the old text
evals.expected_output: Update the golden to match the new callback text — the agent fix is correct

assertions.text: Recognizes the agent fix is intentional and correct
Recommends updating the golden expected text to match the new callback output
Does NOT recommend reverting the callback fix
Mentions re-running with --runs 5 to verify the updated golden passes consistently

evals.prompt: agent makes up responses when the customer is silent on a voice call, look at this
evals.expected_output: Add silence handling callback — detect no-input signal, repeat last message, end after 3 strikes

assertions.text: Identifies the 'no user activity' signal pattern from the platform
Recommends handling silence in before_model_callback, not instruction
Recommends repeating the last agent message on first/second silence
Recommends ending the session after 3 consecutive silences
Does NOT recommend telling the LLM to 'wait for user input'

evals.prompt: greeting golden keeps failing, agent says different things each time
evals.expected_output: Move greeting to before_model_callback for deterministic response

assertions.text: Recommends moving greeting to a callback (before_model_callback)
Explains LLM paraphrases instruction text, callback guarantees exact greeting
References the session start event detection pattern
Does NOT recommend just adding stronger wording to the instruction

evals.prompt: escalation goldens are stuck at 67%, what should i try next
evals.expected_output: Reads experiment_log.md, avoids all 4 previously failed approaches, proposes something new

assertions.text: Reads the experiment log to understand what was already tried
Does NOT recommend simplifying the instruction (failed in iteration 2)
Does NOT recommend hardcoded phrase matching in callbacks (failed in iteration 3)
Does NOT recommend hide_tool() (failed in iteration 4)
Does NOT recommend negative trigger conditions (failed in iteration 5)
Proposes an approach NOT in the experiment log (e.g., check tool availability, trigger pattern, priority ordering)

evals.prompt: i made a change and pass rate dropped from 90% to 60%, what happened
evals.expected_output: Investigate why it regressed, consider reverting, use triage to understand

assertions.text: Investigates why the pass rate dropped (reads triage or transcripts)
Distinguishes between agent regression and infrastructure issues
Mentions reverting or --auto-revert as an option for real regressions
Does NOT recommend pushing forward without understanding the cause

evals.prompt: switch to the humana project
evals.expected_output: Explains .active-project and how to switch projects

assertions.text: Mentions .active-project file as the mechanism for tracking the current project
Explains how to switch projects (update .active-project or use the switch command)

evals.prompt: i need to set up a new voice agent for a bank
evals.expected_output: Copies project template, asks about modality, sets model for voice

assertions.text: Copies the project template to create a new named project folder
Asks about modality (voice vs text)
Sets model to gemini-3.1-flash-live for voice modality
Creates a named project folder for the bank agent

evals.prompt: lint says instruction references a tool not in the agent's tool list
evals.expected_output: Identifies S002, recommends adding tool to agent, explains silent improvisation

assertions.text: Identifies the S002 lint rule as the relevant error
Recommends adding the tool to the agent's tool list (not removing from instruction)
Explains the LLM silently improvises when a referenced tool is missing

evals.prompt: pass rate dropped after my change but triage shows all failures are timeouts
evals.expected_output: Does NOT revert (platform issue), recommends re-running after stabilization

assertions.text: Does NOT recommend reverting the change (failures are platform-side, not agent-side)
Identifies all failures as TIMEOUT / platform infrastructure issues
Recommends re-running evals after the platform stabilizes

evals.prompt: goldens dropped but sims improved after my change
evals.expected_output: Does NOT auto-revert (mixed signal), recommends investigating golden expectations

assertions.text: Does NOT recommend auto-reverting (mixed signal means the change has value)
Recommends investigating whether golden expectations need updating
Notes that sim improvement means real conversations handle better

evals.prompt: i need a text-only chatbot, what model should i use
evals.expected_output: Recommends gemini-3-flash for text, sets modality and default_channel

assertions.text: Recommends gemini-3-flash (not the -live variant) for text-only
Sets modality to text
Sets default_channel to text

evals.prompt: escalation calls the right tool but with wrong arguments, look at this
evals.expected_output: Better docstrings, state fallback, does NOT recommend hide_tool

assertions.text: Recommends better tool docstrings with (REQUIRED) markers
Recommends state fallback in tool code to recover correct values
Does NOT recommend hide_tool()

evals.prompt: agent needs to check if its after business hours and say we're closed. should this be instruction or callback?
evals.expected_output: Recommends callback — time check is deterministic, no LLM judgment needed

assertions.text: Recommends callback (not instruction) for business hours check
Explains time check is deterministic — no LLM judgment needed
Explains flag check + fixed response = callback territory

evals.prompt: golden fails on text but the agent's response is actually better than what the golden expects
evals.expected_output: Confirm agent is correct, update golden text, do NOT lower threshold

assertions.text: Confirms agent behavior is correct first before making changes
Updates golden text to match the improved agent response
Does NOT lower the threshold as the fix

evals.prompt: evals are failing, i just started working on this agent
evals.expected_output: Inspects agent first, then triages, then reads transcripts before diagnosing

assertions.text: Runs inspect-app.py first to understand the agent before diagnosing
Runs triage-results.py to categorize the failures
Reads transcripts before diagnosing root causes

evals.prompt: everything is broken, goldens at 40%, sims at 20%, callbacks failing too
evals.expected_output: Start with callbacks (fastest, most precise), then goldens, then sims

assertions.text: Starts with callback tests first (fastest feedback loop, most precise)
Fixes foundational issues (callbacks) before LLM-dependent ones (goldens, sims)
Addresses goldens before sims (deterministic before open-ended)

evals.prompt: agent keeps ignoring the last few steps in the taskflow
evals.expected_output: Instruction length causes LLM to lose focus on later steps

assertions.text: Identifies instruction length as a possible cause (LLM loses focus on later steps)
Recommends checking instruction length
Suggests moving lower-priority steps to sub-agents or dynamic prompting

evals.prompt: i updated scrapi and now my callbacks are crashing with AttributeError
evals.expected_output: Check for API changes, check changelog, do NOT revert scrapi

assertions.text: Checks for API changes (method renames, attribute changes) in scrapi
Recommends checking the scrapi changelog for breaking changes
Does NOT recommend reverting scrapi as the first fix

evals.prompt: my golden has 5 turns but only the first 3 are deterministic, last 2 vary
evals.expected_output: Truncate golden at turn 3, use sim for remaining turns

assertions.text: Recommends truncating the golden at turn 3 (last deterministic turn)
Recommends using a sim for the remaining non-deterministic turns
Mentions delete+recreate for truncation (update merges, doesn't replace)

evals.prompt: build me an agent for customer support
evals.expected_output: Detects no .venv, runs setup.sh before attempting any build work

assertions.text: Checks for .venv/ existence before routing to build
Runs .agents/skills/cxas-agent-foundry/scripts/setup.sh (not ./setup.sh)
Does NOT attempt to create agents, tools, or evals before setup completes
Activates the virtualenv after setup

evals.prompt: create evals for my agent
evals.expected_output: Detects missing gecx-config.json, runs setup.sh --configure

assertions.text: Detects that .venv/ exists but gecx-config.json is missing
Runs setup.sh --configure (not full setup)
Does NOT reinstall cxas-scrapi when it is already installed
Proceeds to routing after config is created

evals.prompt: run the evals
evals.expected_output: Detects existing setup, skips onboarding, routes directly to run

assertions.text: Does NOT run setup.sh when .venv/ and gecx-config.json both exist
Routes directly to the Run sub-skill
Uses run-all-evals.py or scrapi-eval-runner.py

evals.prompt: i have a PRD, build an agent from it
evals.expected_output: Loads interview-guide first, does NOT load all references upfront

assertions.text: Loads interview-guide.md during requirements gathering
Does NOT load gecx-design-guide.md before starting the interview
Does NOT load api-reference.md before the build step
Does NOT load all 5+ references in the first step

evals.prompt: i just built the agent, is it working?
evals.expected_output: Runs quick verification (lint, smoke test, inspect) not full 7 gates

assertions.text: Runs lint.py as a verification step
Runs a smoke test via Sessions API
Runs inspect-app.py to confirm structure
Mentions full 7-gate verification is available for production readiness

evals.prompt: I made changes locally and also edited the agent on the platform. Now when I push I'm getting conflicts. How do I get back in sync?
evals.expected_output: Recommends cxas pull to reconcile, then merge, then push

assertions.text: Recommends cxas pull before attempting resolution
Explains the pull → merge → push workflow for reconciling diverged state
Does NOT tell the user to force-push or skip reconciliation
Explains platform edits and local edits must be reconciled, not overwritten

evals.prompt: I updated my golden YAML with a Python script and now the eval is broken. The YAML parses fine though.
evals.expected_output: Identifies yaml.dump() as the likely cause — reformats, mangles strings, loses comments

assertions.text: Identifies yaml.dump() as the likely cause (reformats, mangles strings, loses comments)
Recommends targeted edits instead of yaml.dump() for hand-written YAML
Does NOT suggest the YAML is valid just because it parses

evals.prompt: I deleted some failing evals while the run was still going and now my results are showing ERROR state.
evals.expected_output: Identifies deleting evals during active runs as the cause, recommends waiting for COMPLETED

assertions.text: Identifies deleting evals during active runs as the cause of ERROR state
Recommends waiting for run to reach COMPLETED state before deleting evals
Does NOT suggest deleting more evals or force-stopping the run as a fix

evals.prompt: Lint is showing callback errors and warnings. What should I fix first?
evals.expected_output: Interprets each violation, prioritizes errors (C002 wrong args, C003 camelCase) over warnings (C004-C006), suggests re-running lint

assertions.text: Correctly interprets each callback violation code from the lint output
Prioritizes errors (C002 wrong args, C003 camelCase) over warnings (C004 return type, C005 hardcoded phrases, C006 bare except)
Suggests re-running lint after fixes
Does NOT suggest disabling the lint rules

evals.prompt: My agent instructions are triggering a wall of lint errors. Can you help me prioritize which to fix first?
evals.expected_output: Triages by severity — conflicting rules before ambiguity before length

assertions.text: Triages by severity — conflicting rules before ambiguity before length
Does NOT suggest disabling the lint rules
Recommends fixing issues in the instruction, not suppressing warnings

evals.prompt: Lint says my tool definitions have issues. What should I fix first?
evals.expected_output: Prioritizes T001 error (missing agent_action) over T002 warning (missing docstring) over T003/T004 info

assertions.text: Prioritizes T001 (missing agent_action error return) as the most critical fix
Explains that T002 (missing docstring) affects tool routing because CES uses docstrings for invocation decisions
Explains what each tool lint rule checks

evals.prompt: I want to add a custom evaluation criterion that checks whether my agent maintains a professional tone throughout the conversation. How do I set that up?
evals.expected_output: Walks through creating an evaluation expectation with an LLM judge prompt

assertions.text: Walks through creating an evaluation expectation with an LLM judge prompt
References evaluation expectations from creating-evals.md
Creates a specific, testable judge prompt (not vague)

evals.prompt: I added a new tool to my agent but it's not showing up when I test the agent. The tool definition looks correct.
evals.expected_output: Checks deployment status, tool ID correctness, and toolset assignment systematically

assertions.text: Checks deployment status, tool ID correctness, and toolset assignment
Uses inspect-app.py to verify tool associations
Does NOT suggest recreating the tool from scratch as a first step
Identifies common root causes (wrong toolset, undeployed, wrong agent association)

evals.prompt: My scenario evals show tasks as completed even though the agent clearly didn't finish. I checked the taskCompletionBehavior in my config and it's set to TASK_COMPLETION_BEHAVIOR_UNSPECIFIED. Could that be the issue?
evals.expected_output: Identifies taskCompletionBehavior misconfiguration, recommends changing to TASK_SATISFIED

assertions.text: Confirms that TASK_COMPLETION_BEHAVIOR_UNSPECIFIED is the problem
Recommends changing to TASK_SATISFIED
Does NOT suggest adjusting scoring thresholds to compensate
Provides the exact config change needed

evals.prompt: I want to see what the agent actually says for my golden flows before writing the expected text. Is there a way to capture that?
evals.expected_output: Recommends capture-golden-transcripts.py, warns about circular testing

assertions.text: Recommends capture-golden-transcripts.py to replay user turns against the live agent
Warns that captured transcripts should be used as REFERENCE, not copied verbatim as golden expected text
Explains goldens should represent ideal PRD behavior, not current agent behavior
Does NOT suggest using the captured transcript directly as the golden

evals.prompt: My audio evals sound robotic and unnatural. How do I configure the sim user's voice for audio evals?
evals.expected_output: Explains persona speech configuration via app.evaluationPersonas and PATCH

assertions.text: Explains persona configuration lives in app.evaluationPersonas
Shows how to configure persona via app PATCH API
Mentions voice name, language code, and speaking rate as configurable options
Does NOT suggest changing the agent instruction to compensate for voice quality

evals.prompt: My golden tool call keeps failing because the summary arg text varies each run, but the tool call itself is correct. How do I make it less strict?
evals.expected_output: Use $matchType directives — ignore for free-text, semantic for meaning-based, contains for keywords

assertions.text: Recommends $matchType directive on the varying parameter
Recommends 'ignore' for free-text fields like summary
Explains the $matchType/$matchValue/$originalValue JSON structure
Mentions semantic, ignore, contains, and regexp as available match types
Does NOT recommend lowering the app-level scoring threshold as the fix

evals.prompt: My scenario eval is ignoring the session variables I set. The agent acts like it doesn't know the customer is authenticated.
evals.expected_output: Checks variableOverrides placement and format — must be inside scenario object, not top-level

assertions.text: Checks whether variableOverrides is placed inside the scenario object (not top-level)
Verifies variable names match what the before_agent_callback expects
Checks if the callback derives the variable from a profile identifier (override would be skipped)
Does NOT suggest changing the agent instruction to handle missing variables

evals.prompt: My agent keeps saying 'I encountered an error' instead of giving a real response. The tool works fine when I test it directly.
evals.expected_output: Check tool error handling, agent_action return pattern, and session context differences

assertions.text: Checks the tool's error handling and return format (agent_action pattern)
Checks whether the tool works differently in agent context vs direct execution (session variables, auth)
Recommends using execute-tool directly via SCRAPI to isolate the issue
Does NOT recommend changing the agent instruction to suppress error messages

evals.prompt: I need to share eval results with my manager. How do I generate a report that covers both goldens and sims?
evals.expected_output: Uses generate-combined-report.py with --golden-run and --sim-results flags

assertions.text: Recommends generate-combined-report.py for stakeholder-ready reports
Explains the --golden-run and --sim-results flags for combining both eval types
Mentions --golden-modality and --sim-modality flags for audio reports
Notes that run-all-evals.py auto-generates a combined report as well

evals.prompt: I ran the linter but I want a deeper analysis of my instruction quality. Is there a more thorough check?
evals.expected_output: Recommends lint.py --deep for LLM-powered Tier 2 analysis

assertions.text: Recommends running lint.py with --deep flag for LLM-powered analysis
Explains that --deep uses Gemini for instruction quality, progressive disclosure, voice naturalness, and routing completeness checks
Notes that --deep is slower and should be used after fixing Tier 1 (structural) issues first

evals.prompt: My voice agent takes too long to respond. Users are hanging up before the agent finishes. How do I diagnose this?
evals.expected_output: Enable latency reports for audio runs, check tool response times, consider instruction length

assertions.text: Recommends enabling latency reports for audio eval runs (p50/p90/p99 breakdowns)
Checks tool response times as a common cause of high latency
Checks instruction length — longer instructions increase first-response latency
Does NOT recommend just telling the agent to respond faster in the instruction

evals.prompt: my golden auth eval keeps failing even though all the turns match. The triage shows tools=1/1 and all sem scores are 4. What's going on?
evals.expected_output: Identifies EXTRA_TURNS - agent transfers to sub-agent after golden ends, producing extra output the golden doesn't cover

assertions.text: Identifies the extra turns (agent transfer + sub-agent response) as the cause of failure
Explains that all expected turns pass but extra output causes the overall FAIL
Does NOT recommend changing the agent - the agent behavior is correct
Recommends either accepting the golden as auth-only or ending it before the transfer turn

evals.prompt: my greeting golden fails on the custom expectation 'The agent must deliver a warm, natural greeting' but the agent clearly says a greeting on turn 2. Why?
evals.expected_output: Identifies EXPECTATION_FAIL - the LLM judge misinterprets the ambiguous expectation

assertions.text: Identifies the expectation wording as ambiguous - doesn't specify WHEN the greeting should occur
Explains the judge sees the disclosure as the first utterance and evaluates the greeting expectation against it
Recommends rephrasing the expectation to be specific about turn order
Does NOT recommend removing the HIPAA disclosure to fix the expectation
